title: Latent Degradation Trend Features
summary: Recent F1 tyre research models degradation as a latent state evolving over laps with pit stops as resets; current attempts use simple deltas/rolling means but not explicit within-stint trend estimates.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre research models degradation as a latent state evolving over laps with pit stops as resets; current attempts use simple deltas/rolling means but not explicit within-stint trend estimates.
implementation_hint: For each Driver-Race-Stint sorted by LapNumber, fit cheap expanding/rolling slope features: lap_time_slope_3_5, degradation_slope, residual_vs_stint_linear_fit, acceleration_of_laptime, and normalized residual after removing race-lap median pace. Use only past laps via shift/expanding windows.
expected_effect: Better detects tyres falling off before a stop, especially when absolute lap time is confounded by fuel burn and track evolution.
risk: Early-stint rows have missing slopes; improper sorting or unshifted rolling windows can leak current/future information.
